title: Filtering, Three-Valued Logic, Patterns, Ordering, and Pagination · Data Contract Brief: Order Limit Pagination
summary: A queue view must include the correct unresolved tickets, handle missing owners, and remain deterministic across pages. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql order limit pagination increment in a deterministic queue-filter query suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create deterministic multi-key ordering and evaluate offset and keyset pagination tradeoffs.
Apply LIKE and explicit escaping or case rules without confusing substring search with equality.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Return unresolved high-or-critical tickets plus unowned work from the seeded database. Expose ticket_id, a readable owner label, age bucket 2, and deterministic severity/opened ordering; then test a NULL owner and tied timestamp. Competency probe: Create deterministic multi-key ordering and evaluate offset and keyset pagination tradeoffs. A defensible solution applies Order Limit Pagination, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a deterministic queue-filter query suite.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-order-limit-pagination — Create deterministic multi-key ordering and evaluate offset and keyset pagination tradeoffs.
SELECT ticket_id,
       COALESCE(CAST(owner_id AS TEXT), 'unassigned') AS owner,
       CASE severity WHEN 'critical' THEN 1 WHEN 'high' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END AS severity_order
FROM tickets
WHERE resolved_at IS NULL
ORDER BY severity_order, opened_at, ticket_id
LIMIT 6;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: LIMIT alone guarantees repeatable page membership. This fails the Order Limit Pagination contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Order Limit Pagination evidence · Theory Order Limit Pagination
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-order-limit-pagination-1fuu3t" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; predicate, NULL logic, pattern, or deterministic-order evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-order-limit-pagination-1fuu3t
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT ticket_id, owner_id, owner_id = 1 AS owner_is_one
FROM tickets
WHERE owner_id = 1 OR owner_id IS NULL
ORDER BY ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
